instruction: Extract all core computer science concepts from the following lecture JSON. Return only a newline-separated list of unique concepts. Do not explain anything.

text_blocks.text: A function is similar to its mathematical counterpart

runs.text: is similar to its mathematical counterpart

text_blocks.text: Takes in zero or more parameters, processes them in the function body, and returns a result

Imagine going to BestBuyTM and telling them you want your computer fixed.  You are telling them to run a fixing function, with your computer being a parameter.  What you get back is your fixed computer.

runs.text: and telling them you want your computer fixed.  You are telling them to run a fixing function, with your computer being a parameter.  What you get back is your fixed computer.

text_blocks.text: The act of providing parameters in a function call is called Passing-in.

runs.text: The act of providing parameters in a function call is called

text_blocks.text: Java has two kinds of passing-in.

runs.text: Java has two kinds of passing-in.

text_blocks.text: Passing-in a copy of the value of the variable.

runs.text: Passing-in a copy of the value of the variable.

text_blocks.text: Any change made to the variable is not reflected when the function returns.

runs.text: Any change made to the variable is not reflected when the function returns.

text_blocks.text: All primitive type parameters are pass-by-value.

runs.text: All primitive type parameters are pass-by-value.

text_blocks.text: Any change made to the variable IS reflected when the function returns, UNLESS the parameter name is REASSIGNED.

runs.text: Any change made to the variable IS reflected when the function returns, UNLESS the parameter name is REASSIGNED.

text_blocks.text: All non-primitive type parameters are pass-by-reference.

runs.text: All non-primitive type parameters are pass-by-reference.
